channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Over 2300 Indians Safely Brought Back From Iran Since West Asia Conflict Began: MEA
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said on Friday that India has safely brought back home 2,361 people since the beginning of the conflict in West Asia. Addressing a weekly media briefing in New Delhi on Friday, MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal stated that these 2,361 people include Indian students, fishermen and also three foreigners.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Four Maoists, Including Commander Carrying Rs 15 Lakh Reward, Killed In Jharkhand Encounter
item.description: Four dreaded Maoists, including commander Sahdev Mahto, who carried a Rs 15 lakh reward on his head, were killed by security forces in an encounter on the Chatra-Hazaribagh district border on Friday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Chhattisgarh: Maoist Explosives Cache Recovered In Bijapur District
item.description: In a major anti-Maoist operation, security forces recovered a large quantity of explosive materials concealed by Maoists in the dense forests of Annapur under the jurisdiction of Annapur Police Station at Farsegad in Bijapur district.?
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dynamatic Technologies Unveils Plans to develop 2-ton ‘Super-Cheel’ MALE UAV
item.description: Dynamatic Technologies’ CEO and Managing Director Udayant Malhoutra has confirmed that the company is developing an advanced unmanned aerial system (UAS) dubbed Super-Cheel, a 2-tonne-class platform designed to deliver up to 30 hours of continuous flight. The announcement places the Bengaluru-based aerospace firm firmly in the race to develop indigenous Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) UAV capabilities, an area of growing strategic importance for the Indian Armed Forces.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Reorients 30-Year Submarine Plan Toward Nuclear and AIP Platforms
item.description: India’s long-standing 30-Year Submarine Building Plan, formulated in 1999 with the objective of fielding 24 conventional submarines by 2030, is undergoing a significant strategic recalibration. Faced with an evolving two-front maritime threat from China and Pakistan, the Indian Navy is increasingly prioritizing a mix of nuclear-powered and Air Independent Propulsion (AIP)-equipped submarines over traditional diesel-electric platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO Validates Longer Ranged Astra Mk2, Integration Near Completion on Su-30MKI
item.description: India’s next-generation Astra Mk2 beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile (BVRAAM) has reached a critical milestone, with basic integration largely completed across key Indian Air Force platforms. The missile has now been successfully paired with the Su-30MKI’s Bars radar and is also being integrated with the Tejas Mk1A, supported by indigenous data link architecture.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russia Clears Export of R-37M to India as Interim Solution Against Chinese PL-15 and PL-17
item.description: Russia has reportedly cleared the export of its R-37M “Axehead” ultra-long-range air-to-air missile to India, marking a significant development in the evolving air combat balance in South Asia. Reports emerging from March and April 2026 indicate that the Kremlin has granted approval for Russian defence firms to offer the missile to the Indian Air Force (IAF), in what is being seen as a strategic response to recent regional airpower dynamics.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Operation Sindoor: PAF’s ‘Civilian Masking’ Tactic Raised Serious Air Warfare Concerns
item.description: During Operation Sindoor (May 7–10, 2025), the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) is reported to have employed a controversial aerial tactic often described as “Civilian Masking” or “Radar Shadowing,” aimed at complicating India’s air defence response during the opening phase of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Egypt Invites Indian Firm Parason to Boost Local Manufacturing Under Sisi’s Strategic Vision
item.description: India and Egypt are taking another step toward strengthening their growing defence-industrial partnership, as Parason Advanced Systems engaged in high-level discussions with Egypt’s Ministry of Military Production to explore avenues for strategic cooperation across manufacturing sectors.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Information Warfare Unit to Integrate Military, Bureaucracy, Media and Digital Experts
item.description: India is preparing to operationalize a dedicated Information Warfare (IW) Unit that reflects the evolving character of modern conflict, where dominance is no longer confined to land, air, sea, or space but increasingly contested in the information domain. What sets this initiative apart is its hybrid structure, which is expected to include not only uniformed military personnel but also civilian experts drawn from the bureaucracy, media, and digital technology sectors.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russia says in talks with Su-30 Operators for Su-57 Sale
item.description: Russia has officially secured new export contracts for its Su-57E fifth-generation stealth fighter, signaling a significant expansion into the international arms market. On April 15, 2026, during the opening of the DSA-2026 international arms exhibition in Kuala Lumpur, the state arms exporter Rosoboronexport announced that several partners have already signed contracts for the aircraft.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Space Start up Astrobase Space installs its engine test stand
item.description: Astrobase has successfully fabricated and installed its engine test stand that can support upto 200 Tons of upward thrust. The 80 Ton thrust Full Flow Staged Combustion (FFSC) engine will be fired here.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, China Discuss Implementation Of SCO Leaders Decisions, Organisation’s Future Course
item.description: India and China held Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Bilateral Consultations in New Delhi, discussing the implementation of the SCO Leaders’ decisions and the future course of the organisation. The two sides agreed to continue and consolidate mutual cooperation and consultations in SCO matters, according to a statement released by the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) on Friday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Had a very good conversation’: Trump on phone call with ‘friend’ PM Modi
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Friday said he had a “very good conversation” with his “friend” Prime Minister Narendra Modi. He was referring to the talks the two leaders had on Tuesday. “I had a very good talk with him, and he’s a friend of mine from India, and he’s doing great. We had a very good conversation,” Trump told reporters here as he headed to Las Vegas to address a roundtable on tax cuts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India calls for early restoration of freedom of navigation through Hormuz
item.description: India termed the targeting of commercial shipping during the West Asia conflict as deplorable, strongly urging that unimpeded freedom of navigation and global commerce through the Strait of Hormuz be restored at the earliest.
item.dc:creator: admin
